stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • 2023: Researchers in Kenya convened a symposium to address increased crop raiding by African elephants on smallholder farms.
  • Over 480 documented raids in the prior year involved herds trampling fields and consuming maize stores.
  • Studies show that expanding agriculture has reduced elephants’ access to traditional foraging corridors by roughly 70%.
  • Researchers believe the loss of foraging space has driven hungry elephants closer to villages.
  • The symposium recommended pilot deployment of beehive fences, a nonlethal deterrent, pending further field testing.
stem: The student wants to make and support a claim about African elephant behavior. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: As confirmed by the symposium’s review of 480 raids, beehive fences are a proven and effective deterrent against crop-raiding elephants.
The recent rise in crop raiding by African elephants likely reflects food scarcity, as raids have grown more frequent while traditional foraging corridors have shrunk.
In 2023, researchers in Kenya convened a symposium that addressed reports of elephants trampling fields and consuming maize stores.
Maize yields have fallen by approximately 70% on Kenyan smallholder farms because of recent changes in elephant foraging behavior.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. It makes a generalization (crop raiding likely reflects food scarcity) and supports it with note evidence linking corridor loss to increased raiding.

Choice A misrepresents the notes (the symposium only recommended pilot deployment pending further testing). Choice C describes the symposium without making or supporting a claim about behavior. Choice D inverts the causal direction: the notes attribute the 70% figure to corridor loss, not maize yield loss.
